content.text: Wudu (ablution) is the minor ritual purification required for prayer and some other acts of worship. It prepares the believer physically and spiritually.

items: Before the five daily prayers if wudu was broken.
After acts that nullify wudu (natural discharges, deep sleep, loss of consciousness).
Before touching the Mushaf in some schools.
Wash hands to the wrists three times.
Rinse mouth and nose three times.
Wash arms up to the elbows three times (right then left).
Perform masah over the head once.
Wash the feet up to the ankles three times (right then left).
Bleeding or pus in large amounts.

content.text: Wudu is both physical cleansing and a moment of spiritual focus before prayer.
